approved_sources.title: Conservator engineer review
approved_sources.fit: Directly states the optimization goal: stronger regression recall, clearer evidence order, evaluator and validation coverage, stable output, and an explicit no-finding path.
approved_sources.risk_notes: High-authority local source; use as the primary rewrite brief.

approved_sources.title: Current conservator agent contract
approved_sources.fit: Defines the existing review-only scope, required workflow checks, and required output headings that must be preserved or tightened rather than replaced.
approved_sources.risk_notes: Preserve subagent-only role and review-only boundary.

approved_sources.title: Customization tests for conservator and trainer workflow
approved_sources.fit: Pins repo-owned phrases and behavioral requirements around MCP routing, single-shot optimize boundaries, authored eval separation, and validation workflow expectations.
approved_sources.risk_notes: Acts as a contract check for the prompt rewrite and validation.

approved_sources.title: Prompt optimization and eval-manifest instructions
approved_sources.fit: Defines trainer skill separation, dataset boundaries, and prompt-file validation habits that the conservator should reliably inspect.
approved_sources.risk_notes: Use together with eval-manifest guidance to author realistic review cases.

approved_sources.title: Trainer-optimize dataset and runtime contract
approved_sources.fit: Defines the real scoring shapes, hidden evaluator fields, and the fact that the runtime only injects row data through prompt placeholders.
approved_sources.risk_notes: Critical for blocker detection: the raw `.agent.md` target is not optimizer-native.

approved_sources.title: Judge-agent trainer-workspace precedent
approved_sources.fit: Shows the repo-accepted pattern for `.agent.md` targets: synthesize datasets, record optimize blockers locally, and persist a grounded manual rewrite only when the optimizer cannot consume the raw target meaningfully.
approved_sources.risk_notes: Useful process precedent, not a substitute for conservator-specific evidence.

approved_sources.title: RULERS: Locked Rubrics and Evidence-Anchored Scoring for Robust LLM Evaluation
approved_sources.fit: Supports a fixed audit sequence, explicit score boundaries, and evidence-anchored judgments instead of free-form critique.
approved_sources.risk_notes: Use as supporting methodology only; repo-local workflow rules remain primary.

rejected_candidates.title: Generic code-review checklists
rejected_candidates.reason: Too broad to cover repo-specific trainer workflow regressions such as MCP routing, authored eval separation, or optimizer blocker handling.

rejected_candidates.title: Optimizer benchmark results without the repo runtime contract
rejected_candidates.reason: They do not explain the `.agent.md` placeholder mismatch or the repo's requirement to keep run artifacts under the local `.trainer-workspace` tree.

rejected_candidates.title: Blog-style LLM reviewer tips
rejected_candidates.reason: Derivative advice without stable task contracts or observable evaluator rules does not clear the approval bar for authored eval cases.
